title: Run a smoke test of the Phase 0 Handoff Pipeline in ~/Dev/claude-config to verify delegation-context generation works in the live environment. <task> Smoke-test the Phase 0 Handoff Pipeline implementation. Working directory: ~/Dev/claude-config Run the following three tests and report pass/fail for...
summary_text: **Raw Output** Test 1 raw output: ```text /bin/bash: cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted ``` Test 2 raw output: ```text /bin/bash: cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted ``` Test 3 raw output: ```text /bin/bash: cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted ``` **Results** Test 1 - `DIR OK` — FAIL — command did not start; shell here-doc creation was blocked. - `writeDelegationContext exists` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `--auto-context injection` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `redactSecrets from output-filter` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `per-lane file pattern (runId--)` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `byte budget enforcement` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `atomic write pattern` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `--delegation-context-file flag` — FAIL — not evaluated. Test 2 - `stale context check` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `fallback path` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `collectAutoContext modified` — FAIL — not evaluated. Test 3 - `--auto-context occurrences` — FAIL — not evaluated. - `Count` — FAIL — not evaluated. **Interpretation** All three smoke-test commands failed before Node executed. The live environment’s read-only sandbox does not permit the shell to create the temporary file required for the here-doc form `<< 'EOF'`, so this was an execution-environment failure, not a confirmed Phase 0 implementation failure. Confirmed features: none; the scripts never executed, so no Phase 0 handoff features were positively verified. Missing features: none conclusively identified; current result is “not testable in this sandbox,” not “feature absent.” Recommendation: rerun the exact same commands in a shell environment that allows here-doc temp-file creation, then evaluate the printed booleans/occurrence list.
